CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Continued To Jan-27-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JONATHAN PIPER, ESQ. of Lipton & Piper, Tel No. 415-362-6286 appearing for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Vespertine, Inc.;
Atty. PETER W. CLERIDES, ESQ., Tel No. 415-732-1744 appearing for Cross-Defendant CMS Security, Inc..

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to CROSS-DEFENDANT CMS SECURITY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT, the demurrer to the cross-complaint is sustained, with ten days leave to amend. Pursuant to CCP 430.10(g), the court sustains CMS Security, Inc.'s special demurrer to the third cause of action for declaratory relief, with ten days leave to amend, to allow Vespertine, Inc. the opportunity to plead facts that the alleged agreement between Vespertine, Inc. and CMS Security, Inc, is written, oral, or implied by contract. Under Civil Code sections 1624(a)(2) and 2309, the indemnity agreement must be in writing. Vespertine, Inc.'s declaratory relief cause of action depends upon the existence of a written indemnification agreement between the parties. Therefore, the court sustains CMS Security, Inc.'s general demurrer to the third cause of action for declaratory relief, with ten days leave to amend, to allow Vespertine, Inc. the opportunity to plead facts that the alleged agreement between Vespertine, Inc. and CMS Security, Inc, is a written agreement. Because the indemnification agreement must be in writing, Vespertine cannot base its first cause of action for indemnification and its second cause of action for apportionment of fault on the grounds of an oral or implied contract. (Civil Code sections 1624(a)(2) and 2309.) Therefore, the court sustains CMS Security Inc.'s demurrer to the first and second causes of action, with ten days leave to amend, to allege the existence of a written agreement or an alternative viable basis for liability. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
